name: Category Group Left Bar Visual Treatment

stories.title: Replace category color dot with full-height left bar
acceptanceCriteria: The .nestedGroupDot span is removed from the GroupRenderer depth 1+ render path in ShoppingList.jsx.
A left vertical color bar is rendered on the category block. The bar color matches group.color ?? '#9e9e9e'.
The bar spans the full height of the category block — from the category label row through all item rows beneath it.
The bar is 3px wide.
The category label text is no longer preceded by a color dot.
The .nestedGroupDot CSS class is removed or emptied in ShoppingList.module.css.
The top-level store group header (depth 0) is visually unchanged — its .topLevelDot and card treatment are untouched.
When a category has zero items, the bar height is at least the height of the .nestedGroupTitle row (no zero-height bar).
The .nestedGroupTitle left padding is removed or set to 0 — horizontal spacing from bar to label is controlled entirely by .nestedGroup's padding-left.
npm run lint passes with no new errors.
npm run build succeeds.

stories.title: Indent items 12px from the left bar within a category block
acceptanceCriteria: All item rows rendered inside a category block (depth 1+ GroupRenderer) are indented 12px from the left edge of the category bar.
The indent is applied via CSS padding-left on the items container, not via inline styles.
The indent does not apply to top-level store group items (depth 0 ungrouped items or items in a store with no sub-categories).
On viewports ≤ 480px the indent remains 12px (no reduction).
Item content (checkbox, label, price) is not clipped or overflowed due to the indent.
npm run lint passes with no new errors.
npm run build succeeds.

stories.title: Visual QA: category bar renders correctly across list configurations
acceptanceCriteria: A store group with a single category: bar renders, items indented.
A store group with multiple categories: each category has its own bar in its own color; bars do not bleed into adjacent categories.
A store group with no sub-categories (items directly under store, no category): no bar is rendered, no unexpected indent.
The 'Unassigned' ungrouped items block (top-level, no category): no bar, no indent change.
Dark mode: bar color is visible against the dark background (color is data-driven from group.color, no override needed — verify visually).
iOS (Capacitor shell): bar and indent render correctly at 390px viewport width (iPhone 14 Pro).
